concept_name: Parallel-Plate Capacitor Field — Uniform E = V/d Between the Plates, ≈0 Outside
source_book: NCERT Class 12 Physics, Ch.2 Electrostatic Potential and Capacitance (§2.12 Parallel plate capacitor) — table-of-contents + Indian-anchor reference only; teaching authored from first principles. HC Verma consulted for SEQUENCE (set up two oppositely charged plates with a gap d → switch on V and PREDICT the field shape, confronting the radial-fan instinct → straight parallel equally-spaced lines from + to −, E = V/d → probe at three points and PREDICT the magnitudes, confronting the stronger-near-the-plates instinct → all three equal: the field is UNIFORM → derive WHY from two charged sheets, each σ/2ε₀, adding inside to σ/ε₀ and cancelling outside → turn edge-on and see the field CONFINED to the gap, ≈0 outside with only an edge fringe → hold V fixed and double d, confronting the field-depends-only-on-V instinct → E halves, E ∝ 1/d → hand the explorer the V and d sliders plus a draggable test charge), never copied. The atomic idea: between two oppositely charged parallel plates the electric field is UNIFORM — straight, parallel, equally-spaced lines running + to −, the SAME magnitude E = V/d = σ/ε₀ at every interior point regardless of position; it is ≈0 outside the plates (the two sheet-fields cancel) with only a small edge fringe; and at fixed voltage E ∝ 1/d. SCOPE: this is the UNIFORM-FIELD diamond of Ch.2. It declares electric_field_point_charge (the radial 1/r² field it is NOT), gauss_law_sheet (the single isolated sheet's σ/2ε₀), and electric_potential_meaning (V = W/q) as prerequisites and does NOT re-teach them. It STOPS SHORT of capacitance C = ε₀A/d, the stored energy ½CV², dielectrics, and the energy density of the field. Conceptual-only (no mode_overrides), EPIC-L-first (no epic_c_branches; the four misconceptions are confronted inside EPIC-L via Rule 16a misconception_watch + predict→reveal). Routed to the field_3d parallel_plates scenario (NEW plate + uniform-field-line + three-probe-arrow + two-sheet-superposition + edge-on-fringe + gap-widen + draggable-test-charge primitives — the field_3d_config below is the renderer contract).

real_world_anchor.primary: Open the regulator knob box of an old ceiling fan and you find a small capacitor: two flat metal sheets held a tiny even gap apart. Put a voltage across them and the space in that gap fills with an electric field that is the SAME everywhere inside — straight, even, edge to edge. That perfectly even field is exactly what we build here: two plates, one small uniform gap.
real_world_anchor.secondary: The screen of your smartphone is a stack of two thin conducting layers separated by a whisker-thin gap. Your fingertip changes the voltage across them, and the touch sensor reads the uniform field in that gap. Two parallel conducting sheets, an even field between — the same physics as the fan capacitor, shrunk onto glass.

sigma.name: Surface charge density on EACH plate

E.name: Electric field magnitude between the plates — uniform

formulas.direction: from the positive plate to the negative plate, perpendicular to both plates

constraints: The electric field between two oppositely charged parallel plates is UNIFORM: the same magnitude and direction at every interior point (C1).
The field lines are straight, parallel, equally spaced and perpendicular to both plates, running from the positive plate to the negative plate (C2).
The field magnitude E does NOT depend on position between the plates — it is independent of how near or far you are from either plate (C3).
Outside an ideal pair of plates the field is approximately zero; only a small fringe field curls around the very edges (C4).
Each plate acts as a charged sheet producing a uniform field of magnitude sigma/(2*epsilon0) on each side (C5).
Between the plates the two sheet-fields point the SAME way and ADD; outside they point OPPOSITE ways and CANCEL (C6).
Inside the plates E = V/d = sigma/epsilon0 — the two expressions agree (C7).
At fixed voltage V, the field is inversely proportional to the separation: E ∝ 1/d (doubling d halves E) (C8).
At fixed separation d, the field is proportional to the voltage: E ∝ V (C9).

aha_moment.statement: The field is the same at every point between the plates.
aha_moment.visual_confirmation: Three E arrows probed at the +edge, the dead centre, and the −edge are drawn identical in length and direction.

STATE_1.title: Two plates, one even gap — the setup

scene_composition.text: left plate +, right plate − — equal and opposite
the gap between them is the separation d
exactly the plates inside a ceiling-fan regulator capacitor

tts_sentences.text_en: Here are two flat metal plates facing each other. The left plate is charged positive, the right plate negative — equal and opposite.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यहाँ दो flat metal plates एक-दूसरे के सामने हैं। left plate positively charged है, right plate negative — equal और opposite।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇక్కడ రెండు flat metal plates ఒకదానికొకటి ఎదురుగా ఉన్నాయి. left plate positive గా charged, right plate negative — equal మరియు opposite.

tts_sentences.text_en: The gap between them is the separation d. There is no field drawn yet — just the two charged plates set a distance apart.
tts_sentences.text_hi: उनके बीच का gap ही separation d है। अभी कोई field नहीं दिखाया गया — बस दो charged plates एक distance d पर रखी हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: వాటి మధ్య gap అనేది separation d. ఇంకా ఏ field చూపించలేదు — కేవలం రెండు charged plates ఒక distance దూరంగా ఉన్నాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: This is exactly the pair of metal sheets inside a ceiling-fan regulator capacitor — two plates, a small even gap.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यह बिल्कुल वही pair है जो ceiling-fan regulator के capacitor के अंदर होती है — दो plates, एक छोटा even gap।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇది సరిగ్గా ఒక ceiling-fan regulator capacitor లోపల ఉండే metal sheets pair — రెండు plates, ఒక చిన్న even gap.

STATE_2.title: Switch on V — does the field fan out, or stay parallel?

scene_composition.text: connect the voltage V — a field appears in the gap
fan out radially, or stay straight and parallel?
straight, parallel, equally spaced — all + to −
their strength is E = V / d

tts_sentences.text_en: Now connect the voltage V across the plates. An electric field appears in the gap between them.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब voltage V को plates के across connect कीजिए। उनके बीच gap में electric field E प्रकट होता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు voltage V ను plates అంతటా connect చేయండి. వాటి మధ్య gap లో electric field E ప్రత్యక్షమవుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Predict its shape: does the field fan out radially, like around a point charge, or stay straight and parallel? Decide before I reveal it.
tts_sentences.text_hi: इसकी shape predict कीजिए: क्या field radially fan out होती है, जैसे point charge के आसपास, या straight और parallel रहती है? reveal करने से पहले decide कीजिए।
tts_sentences.text_te: దాని shape ని predict చేయండి: field radially fan out అవుతుందా, point charge చుట్టూ లాగా, లేక straight మరియు parallel గా ఉంటుందా? నేను reveal చేసే ముందు decide చేయండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: It is straight, parallel, equally-spaced lines, all running the same way — from the positive plate to the negative plate.
tts_sentences.text_hi: ये straight, parallel, equally-spaced lines हैं, सब एक ही direction में चलती हैं — positive plate से negative plate तक।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇవి straight, parallel, equally-spaced lines, అన్నీ ఒకే direction లో నడుస్తున్నాయి — positive plate నుండి negative plate వరకు.

tts_sentences.text_en: Their strength is the electric field E equals voltage V divided by separation d.
tts_sentences.text_hi: उनकी strength electric field E है जो voltage V को separation d से divide करने पर मिलती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: వాటి strength electric field E అనేది voltage V ని separation d తో divide చేయడం వల్ల వస్తుంది.

misconception_watch.belief: The field between the plates spreads out radially, fanning from each plate the way it does around a point charge.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: The lines fade in dead straight, parallel and equally spaced, every one running plate-to-plate in the same direction — no fan, no curve.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Between parallel plates the field lines are straight, parallel and equally spaced from + to −, not radial.

STATE_3.title: Probe three points — stronger near the plates, or all equal?

scene_composition.text: probe at +edge, dead centre, and −edge
stronger near the plates, or the same everywhere?
all three arrows identical — same size, same direction
UNIFORM: E does not depend on where you stand

tts_sentences.text_en: Let me probe the field at three places: right next to the positive plate, in the dead centre, and next to the negative plate.
tts_sentences.text_hi: तीन जगहों पर field probe करते हैं: positive plate के बिल्कुल पास, dead centre में, और negative plate के पास।
tts_sentences.text_te: మూడు చోట్ల field ని probe చేద్దాం: positive plate దగ్గరగా, dead centre లో, మరియు negative plate దగ్గర.

tts_sentences.text_en: Predict the three arrow lengths: is the electric field E stronger near the plates, or exactly the same at every point in between?
tts_sentences.text_hi: तीन arrows की lengths predict कीजिए: क्या electric field E plates के पास stronger है, या बीच के हर point पर exactly same है?
tts_sentences.text_te: మూడు arrows యొక్క lengths ని predict చేయండి: electric field E plates దగ్గర stronger గా ఉందా, లేక మధ్యలో ప్రతి point వద్ద exactly same గా ఉందా?

tts_sentences.text_en: All three arrows are identical. The field has the same magnitude and the same direction at every interior point.
tts_sentences.text_hi: तीनों arrows identical हैं। field की magnitude और direction हर interior point पर same है।
tts_sentences.text_te: మూడు arrows ఒకేలా ఉన్నాయి. field యొక్క magnitude మరియు direction ప్రతి interior point వద్ద same గా ఉంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: That is what UNIFORM means — electric field E does not depend on where you stand between the plates.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यही UNIFORM का मतलब है — electric field E इस पर depend नहीं करता कि आप plates के बीच कहाँ खड़े हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: UNIFORM అంటే అదే అర్థం — electric field E మీరు plates మధ్య ఎక్కడ నిలుచున్నారు అన్నదానిపై depend చేయదు.

misconception_watch.belief: The field is stronger right next to the plates and weaker in the middle of the gap.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: Three probe arrows at the +edge, the centre, and the −edge are drawn identical in length and direction — the field never changes across the gap.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: The field between parallel plates is uniform — E = V/d is the same at every interior point.

STATE_4.title: Why uniform — two sheets, σ/2ε₀ each, adding inside

scene_composition.text: each plate is a sheet: field σ / 2ε₀, same at any distance
between the plates the two sheet-fields ADD
outside they point opposite ways and CANCEL
inside: E = σ / ε₀ = V / d — the same uniform field

tts_sentences.text_en: Here is why. Treat each plate as a charged sheet. One sheet alone makes a uniform field of size surface charge density sigma over two epsilon-nought — the same at any distance.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यह रहा कारण। हर plate को एक charged sheet मानिए। एक sheet अकेले surface charge density σ over two ε₀ का uniform field बनाती है — किसी भी distance पर same।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇందుకే. ప్రతి plate ని ఒక charged sheet గా treat చేయండి. ఒక sheet మాత్రమే surface charge density σ over two ε₀ పరిమాణంలో uniform field చేస్తుంది — ఏ distance లో అయినా same.

tts_sentences.text_en: Between the plates the two sheet-fields point the SAME way, so they add: sigma over two epsilon-nought plus sigma over two epsilon-nought.
tts_sentences.text_hi: plates के बीच दोनों sheet-fields एक ही direction में point करती हैं, इसलिए वे add होती हैं: σ over two ε₀ plus σ over two ε₀।
tts_sentences.text_te: plates మధ్య రెండు sheet-fields ఒకే direction లో point చేస్తాయి, కాబట్టి అవి add అవుతాయి: σ over two ε₀ plus σ over two ε₀.

tts_sentences.text_en: Outside, the two sheet-fields point OPPOSITE ways, so they cancel to nearly zero.
tts_sentences.text_hi: बाहर, दोनों sheet-fields OPPOSITE directions में point करती हैं, इसलिए वे nearly zero हो जाती हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: బయట, రెండు sheet-fields OPPOSITE directions లో point చేస్తాయి, కాబట్టి అవి nearly zero కి cancel అవుతాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Inside, that sum is the electric field E equals surface charge density sigma over epsilon-nought — the very same uniform field as V divided by d.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अंदर, उनका sum electric field E equals surface charge density σ over ε₀ है — V divided by d के same uniform field।
tts_sentences.text_te: లోపల, ఆ sum electric field E equals surface charge density σ over ε₀ — V divided by d తో సరిగ్గా అదే uniform field.

STATE_5.title: Edge-on — the field is confined to the gap

scene_composition.text: turn the capacitor edge-on, look from the side
between the plates: dense, uniform — the field lives here
outside: almost no lines — E ≈ 0, field confined
only a small fringe curls round the edges

tts_sentences.text_en: Turn the capacitor edge-on so we can look from the side.
tts_sentences.text_hi: capacitor को edge-on घुमाइए ताकि हम side से देख सकें।
tts_sentences.text_te: capacitor ని edge-on తిప్పండి తద్వారా మనం side నుండి చూడవచ్చు.

tts_sentences.text_en: Between the plates the field lines are dense and uniform — the field lives here.
tts_sentences.text_hi: plates के बीच field lines dense और uniform हैं — field यहीं रहता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: plates మధ్య field lines dense మరియు uniform గా ఉన్నాయి — field ఇక్కడే నివసిస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Outside the plates there are almost no lines: the electric field E is approximately zero. The field is confined to the gap.
tts_sentences.text_hi: plates के बाहर लगभग कोई lines नहीं हैं: electric field E approximately zero है। field gap तक ही confined है।
tts_sentences.text_te: plates బయట దాదాపు ఏ lines లేవు: electric field E approximately zero గా ఉంది. field gap లోనే confined అయి ఉంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Only a small fringe curls round the very edges — the one place a real capacitor leaks a little field.
tts_sentences.text_hi: केवल एक छोटा fringe किनारों के चारों ओर curl करता है — यही वह जगह है जहाँ real capacitor थोड़ा field leak करता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: కేవలం ఒక చిన్న fringe అంచుల చుట్టూ curl అవుతుంది — real capacitor కొంచెం field leak చేసే ఒకే ఒక చోటు.

misconception_watch.belief: A capacitor's field spreads out into the whole room, like the field of an isolated charge.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: The side view shows dense lines packed inside the gap and essentially nothing outside, with only a faint fringe curling at the edges.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: The field is confined to the gap; outside an ideal capacitor the two plates' fields cancel to nearly zero.

STATE_6.title: Hold V, double d — does E stay, double, or halve?

scene_composition.text: keep V fixed at 12 V, pull the plates twice as far apart
V fixed, d doubled: E stays, doubles, or halves?
gap widens, lines spread, E readout drops to half
E = V / d, so doubling d halves E: E ∝ 1/d

tts_sentences.text_en: Keep the voltage V fixed at twelve volts. I am about to pull the plates twice as far apart, doubling the separation d.
tts_sentences.text_hi: voltage V को बारह volts पर fixed रखिए। मैं plates को दोगुनी दूरी पर खींचने वाला हूँ, separation d को double करते हुए।
tts_sentences.text_te: voltage V ని twelve volts వద్ద fixed గా ఉంచండి. నేను plates ని రెండింతలు దూరంగా లాగబోతున్నాను, separation d ని double చేస్తూ.

tts_sentences.text_en: Predict the electric field E: with voltage V held fixed and separation d doubled, does E stay the same, double, or halve?
tts_sentences.text_hi: electric field E predict कीजिए: voltage V fixed रहे और separation d double हो जाए, तो क्या E same रहता है, double होता है, या half हो जाता है?
tts_sentences.text_te: electric field E ని predict చేయండి: voltage V fixed గా ఉండి separation d double అయితే, E same గా ఉంటుందా, double అవుతుందా, లేక half అవుతుందా?

tts_sentences.text_en: Watch — the gap widens, the field lines spread out, and the live readout of electric field E drops to half its value.
tts_sentences.text_hi: देखिए — gap चौड़ा होता है, field lines फैल जाती हैं, और electric field E का live readout आधा हो जाता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: చూడండి — gap వెడల్పవుతుంది, field lines వ్యాపిస్తాయి, మరియు electric field E యొక్క live readout సగానికి తగ్గుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Because E equals V divided by d, at fixed voltage doubling the separation halves the field: E is proportional to one over d.
tts_sentences.text_hi: क्योंकि E equals V divided by d, fixed voltage पर separation double करने से field half हो जाती है: E proportional है one over d के।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఎందుకంటే E equals V divided by d, fixed voltage వద్ద separation double చేయడం వల్ల field half అవుతుంది: E proportional to one over d.

misconception_watch.belief: The field depends only on the voltage, so moving the plates apart at fixed V leaves E unchanged.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: With V held at 12 V, doubling d spreads the lines apart and the live E readout drops from 1200 to 600 V/m.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Since E = V/d, at fixed voltage doubling the separation halves the field — E ∝ 1/d.

STATE_7.title: You drive — sliders for V and d, drag the test charge

scene_composition.text: drag V and d — watch E = V / d update live
drag the yellow charge inside: force is the same everywhere
drag it outside: the force vanishes — no field out there

tts_sentences.text_en: Now you drive it. Use the sliders to change the voltage V and the separation d, and watch the live electric field E equals V over d update.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब आप drive कीजिए। voltage V और separation d बदलने के लिए sliders use कीजिए, और live electric field E equals V over d update होते देखिए।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు మీరు drive చేయండి. voltage V మరియు separation d మార్చడానికి sliders use చేయండి, మరియు live electric field E equals V over d update అవడం చూడండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Drag the yellow test charge anywhere inside: the force on it stays the same size everywhere, because the field is uniform.
tts_sentences.text_hi: yellow test charge को अंदर कहीं भी drag कीजिए: उस पर force हर जगह same size का रहता है, क्योंकि field uniform है।
tts_sentences.text_te: yellow test charge ని లోపల ఎక్కడైనా drag చేయండి: దానిపై force ప్రతిచోటా same size గా ఉంటుంది, ఎందుకంటే field uniform.

tts_sentences.text_en: Drag it outside the plates and the force vanishes — there is essentially no field out there.
tts_sentences.text_hi: इसे plates के बाहर drag कीजिए और force vanish हो जाता है — बाहर essentially कोई field नहीं है।
tts_sentences.text_te: దాన్ని plates బయటికి drag చేయండి మరియు force vanish అవుతుంది — బయట essentially ఏ field లేదు.

assessment.mastery_definition: A student has mastered the parallel-plate capacitor field when they can (1) state that the field lines between two oppositely charged parallel plates are straight, parallel and equally spaced, running from the positive plate to the negative plate; (2) recognise that the field is UNIFORM — the same magnitude E = V/d at every interior point, independent of position; (3) reason that at fixed voltage E ∝ 1/d, so doubling the separation halves the field; (4) state that the field outside an ideal capacitor is approximately zero, with only a small edge fringe; (5) derive E = σ/ε₀ between the plates by superposing two charged-sheet fields of σ/2ε₀ each (adding inside, cancelling outside); and (6) conclude that the force F = qE on a test charge is the same everywhere inside, because the field is uniform.

questions.stem: A voltage is connected across two parallel plates. What is the shape of the electric field lines between them?

options.A: Straight, parallel, equally-spaced lines running from the positive plate to the negative plate
options.B: Lines that fan out radially from each plate, like the field around a point charge
options.C: Curved lines that loop from one plate and return to the same plate
options.D: Lines that run along the plates, parallel to their surfaces

distractor_misconceptions.B: imports the radial point-charge picture instead of the straight, parallel plate picture
distractor_misconceptions.C: borrows the closed-loop look of magnetic field lines, which do not apply between charged plates
distractor_misconceptions.D: puts the field ALONG the plates instead of across the gap from + to −

questions.tested_idea: Between parallel plates the field lines are straight, parallel and equally spaced, from + to −.
questions.parallel_form_stem: Two oppositely charged metal plates face each other with a voltage across them. Describe the geometry of the field lines in the gap.

questions.stem: You measure the electric field at three points between the plates — next to the positive plate, in the dead centre, and next to the negative plate. How do the three values compare?

options.A: All three are equal — the field is the same at every interior point
options.B: The field is strongest next to the plates and weakest in the centre
options.C: The field is strongest in the centre and weakest near the plates
options.D: The field falls off steadily from the positive plate to the negative plate

distractor_misconceptions.B: the confronted misconception — imagines the field is concentrated near the plates rather than uniform
distractor_misconceptions.C: inverts that wrong picture, putting the strong field in the middle instead
distractor_misconceptions.D: treats the gap like a 1/r decay away from the positive plate, ignoring uniformity

questions.tested_idea: The field between parallel plates is uniform — equal magnitude at every interior point.
questions.parallel_form_stem: A probe reads the field just inside the positive plate, halfway across, and just inside the negative plate of a capacitor. State how the three readings compare.

questions.stem: The voltage across a parallel-plate capacitor is held fixed while the plates are pulled twice as far apart. What happens to the field between them?

options.A: It halves, because E = V/d and d has doubled
options.B: It stays the same, because the field depends only on the voltage
options.C: It doubles, because the plates are now further apart
options.D: It drops to zero, because the plates are too far apart to influence each other

distractor_misconceptions.B: the confronted misconception — thinks E depends only on V, ignoring the d in E = V/d
distractor_misconceptions.C: gets the proportionality backwards, treating E as growing with d
distractor_misconceptions.D: imagines the field switching off entirely once the gap widens

questions.tested_idea: At fixed voltage E = V/d ∝ 1/d, so doubling the separation halves the field.
questions.parallel_form_stem: Keeping the battery voltage constant, you move the plates of a capacitor from 1 mm to 2 mm apart. State what happens to the field in the gap and why.

questions.stem: What is the electric field in the region OUTSIDE an ideal parallel-plate capacitor, beyond the plates?

options.A: Approximately zero — the two plates' fields cancel outside, leaving only a small edge fringe
options.B: The same uniform field as inside, spreading out into the whole room
options.C: Stronger than inside, because the field escapes the narrow gap
options.D: Half the inside field, because only one plate contributes outside

distractor_misconceptions.B: the confronted misconception — imagines the field leaking out into the surrounding space
distractor_misconceptions.C: thinks the confined field intensifies once it leaves the gap
distractor_misconceptions.D: misreads the cancellation as a halving rather than near-total cancellation

questions.tested_idea: Outside an ideal capacitor the two sheet-fields cancel, so E ≈ 0 except for an edge fringe.
questions.parallel_form_stem: Standing just beyond the edge of a charged parallel-plate capacitor, what electric field would you measure, and why?

questions.stem: Treat each plate as a charged sheet that alone produces a field of magnitude σ/(2ε₀). What is the field BETWEEN the plates, and why?

options.A: σ/ε₀, because the two sheet-fields point the same way between the plates and add
options.B: σ/(2ε₀), because only one plate's field exists between the plates
options.C: Zero, because the two sheet-fields cancel between the plates
options.D: 2σ/ε₀, because the two fields add and then double again across the gap

distractor_misconceptions.B: counts only one sheet, forgetting both plates contribute inside
distractor_misconceptions.C: swaps the regions — cancellation happens OUTSIDE, not between the plates
distractor_misconceptions.D: double-counts the superposition, inflating the result

questions.tested_idea: Between the plates the two σ/2ε₀ sheet-fields add to σ/ε₀; outside they cancel.
questions.parallel_form_stem: Each plate of a capacitor is a sheet making a field σ/2ε₀ on each side. By superposition, what is the field in the gap between the two plates?

questions.stem: A small positive test charge is placed at several points between the plates and the force on it is measured each time. How does the force compare from point to point?

options.A: The same size and direction everywhere between the plates, because the field is uniform
options.B: Larger near the plates and smaller in the middle, where the field is weaker
options.C: Larger in the middle, where the charge is equidistant from both plates
options.D: It changes direction depending on which plate the charge is nearer to

distractor_misconceptions.B: assumes a non-uniform field, so the force would vary with position
distractor_misconceptions.C: imagines the field peaking in the centre rather than being constant
distractor_misconceptions.D: thinks the force direction tracks the nearer plate, not the fixed + to − field

questions.tested_idea: In a uniform field F = qE is constant, so the force on a test charge is the same everywhere inside.
questions.parallel_form_stem: A positive test charge is moved around inside a parallel-plate capacitor. State how the force on it changes as it moves, and justify your answer from the field.

capacitor_defaults.scene_separation_per_state_units: demo units (3D scene), distinct from the physical d in metres

STATE_1.label: Setup — two plates, one even gap d, no field yet

STATE_2.label: Switch on V — straight parallel lines + to −, E = V/d

STATE_3.label: PRIMARY aha — three equal probe arrows: field is uniform
STATE_3.formula_overlay: E is the same at every interior point

STATE_4.label: Why uniform — two sheets σ/2ε₀ add inside, cancel outside
STATE_4.formula_overlay: σ/2ε₀ + σ/2ε₀ = σ/ε₀ inside; cancel outside

STATE_5.label: Edge-on — field confined to the gap, E ≈ 0 outside, small fringe
STATE_5.formula_overlay: field confined to the gap; E ≈ 0 outside

STATE_6.label: Hold V, double d — gap widens, E readout drops to half
STATE_6.formula_overlay: E = V / d → halves when d doubles  (E ∝ 1/d)

STATE_7.label: Explore — sliders V and d, drag the test charge
STATE_7.formula_overlay: E = V / d  (drag V, d, and the test charge)
